element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic for healing and offense throughout game
Lloyd lacks psychic powers, relies on physical attacks
Ana has inherent psychic powers like Ninten
Primary physical weapons; subversion of traditional RPG swords
Persistent threat mechanic throughout overworld exploration
Primary narrative and resource discovery mechanism
Thematic enemy types increasing in difficulty
Unique enemy archetypes subverting fantasy expectations
Fast travel between major area clusters
Modern convenience subversion - calling father instead of priest
Health restoration alternative to magic healing

typical_tension_triggers: Resource scarcity (low HP/PP in isolated areas)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-based tension with frequent random encounters creating unpredictable rhythm; relief cycles tied to reaching safe town structures; overall difficulty curve creates mid-to-late game tension escalation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Dense town exploration with hidden NPCs and items; cave/dungeon optional branches
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Checking/talking with inanimate objects reveals hidden items and story details (parody of genre conventions)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle learning curve with safe town zones and manageable random encounters; introduces mechanics gradually through party recruitment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating encounter difficulty; terrain variety increases (deserts, swamps, forests); paranormal phenomena introduction
difficulty_curve.late_game: Significant difficulty spike with robot and alien-heavy encounters; resource management becomes critical; final bosses represent major power thresholds
notable_spikes: Transition from towns to wilderness areas
Late-game robot and alien invasion sequence
Criticized for high difficulty and balance issues throughout

notable_level_patterns.description: Connected overworld with towns serving as safe zones and narrative anchors; all areas exist in single continuous world (no separate world map disconnection)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ninten's hometown, subsequent towns (Podunk, Onett equivalent)

notable_level_patterns.description: Overworld navigation between towns through deserts, swamps, forests, caves; random encounter pressure increases distance from town safety
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mountain passes, swamp regions, forest areas

notable_level_patterns.description: Laboratories and warehouses instead of fantasy dungeons; modern-day location styling with contained boss encounters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Various facilities housing paranormal phenomena sources

notable_level_patterns.description: Oblique projection 3D-like overworld aesthetic shifts to first-person perspective menu-based combat for encounters
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any random or boss encounter transition
